rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA LAIR ET AL VS. CABLE CAR HOTEL, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Appointment Of Appraiser
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, January 30, 2026, Line 1 PETITIONERs CLAUDIA LAIR, and TONIA LAIR's Motion For Order Requiring Appointment Of Appraiser. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, January 30, 2026, Line 3. 2 - DEFENDANT ARI QAYUMI's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The unopposed motion is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. Off calendar as moot. Second Amended Complaint filed on January 9, 2026, and supersedes the motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication. (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) Defendants' remedy is to seek a trial continuance and refile their motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication in response to the Second Amended Complaint. rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE TYLER VS. B&G FOODS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, January 30, 2026, Line 11. 2 - PLAINTIFF KATHERINE TYLER's Motion For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted. (CCP 128.5; 128.7.) Defendant's insistence on including irrelevant and besmirching allegations in its denial is frivolous and a waste of counsel and court resources. The introduction of the "Second Amended General Denial" violated this court's prior order and there was no legal basis to include the allegations. Plaintiff complied with the safe harbor provision for sanctions and prior to this hearing, defendant did not seek leave to file an amended pleading or withdraw the Second Amended General Denial. Counsel Kwasniewski shall remit $5,000 as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff by March 2, 2026. Counsel shall self-report this sanctions order to the State Bar by March 2, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE TYLER VS. B&G FOODS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, January 30, 2026, Line 11. 1 - PLAINTIFF KATHERINE TYLER's MOTION TO STRIKE. Plaintiff's motion to strike the introduction in the "Second Amended General Denial" is granted without leave to amend. Per CCP 436(a), the court may "[s]trike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter" inserted in the answer/denial. As the court previously ruled, the statements regarding defendant's business ("delicious food"), plaintiffs' counsel's work on a sex exploitation case, prior Proposition 65 litigation, etc. are legally irrelevant to the germane issues in this case. Since the introduction (an attempt to sully plaintiff and counsel) is unnecessary, the court strikes the allegations without leave to amend. Plaintiff correctly notes that "anyone can bring a Proposition 65 lawsuit against the seller of products containing chemicals that may cause cancer or reproductive toxicity without purchasing anything from the seller or suffering any individual harm." (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. Walmart, Inc. (2025) 112 Cal.App.5th 679, 689). While Proposition 65 vindicates public rights, there is no "public interest" defense nor can relief be denied because plaintiff's counsel allegedly engaged in prior misconduct. (Id.; Health & Safety Code 25249.7(d).) The issue is whether defendant's product contains a listed chemical and whether a proper warning was issued. The Attorney General's review of the certificate of merit vets whether the claim is in the public interest. (Health & Safety Code 25249.7(d), (e).) The information in the introduction is unnecessary for the court to determine if a subsequent settlement (if any) is in the public interest/fair. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE TYLER VS. B&G FOODS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SANCTIONS.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, January 30, 2026, Line 12 2 - MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted. (CCP 128.5; 128.7.) Defendant's insistence on including irrelevant and besmirching allegations in its denial is frivolous and a waste of counsel and court resources. The introduction of the "Second Amended General Denial" violated this court's prior order and there was no legal basis to include the allegations. Plaintiff complied with the safe harbor provision for sanctions and prior to this hearing, defendant did not seek leave to file an amended pleading or withdraw the Second Amended General Denial. Counsel Kwasniewski shall remit $5,000 as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff by March 2, 2026. Counsel shall self-report this sanctions order to the State Bar by March 2, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
